mechanism: AI productivity gains (estimated 0.5-1.0%/year) could potentially substitute for the missing labor force growth channel (~0.3%/year drag), restoring some deleveraging capacity. But technology adoption is endogenous, slow, and has a base rate of ~2 sustained accelerations in 150 years.
regime_dependence: AI adoption is endogenous to scarcity (Japan robot density 2x OECD). The offset depends on AI deployment at scale, which is uncertain on investment-relevant horizons. Base rate for technology-driven productivity accelerations lasting >10 years: ~2 in 150 years.
